name: Architecture Spike
description: Time-boxed investigation into an architectural question or technology choice. Produces a structured options analysis and a clear decision with rationale. Ideal for de-risking high-uncertainty design choices before committing to implementation.

stages.name: Problem Framing
stages.description: Define the architectural question being investigated, the constraints and success criteria, and the scope of the spike (what is in-scope and explicitly out-of-scope).

stages.name: Option Analysis
stages.description: Research at least two viable approaches. For each option: describe the approach, evaluate against the success criteria, identify trade-offs, and estimate implementation effort.

stages.name: Decision
stages.description: Select the recommended option. Produce a formal Architecture Decision Record (ADR) capturing the decision, rationale, alternatives considered, and expected impact.

stages.name: Done
stages.description: Spike is complete. The ADR is the primary deliverable.

approvalCheckpoints.description: Human owner must agree the problem is correctly framed before analysis begins.
Human owner must approve the final ADR before the spike is closed.
